title: Diogenes Laertius: Life of Socrates
description: From Diogenes Laertius, Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers — our main source for the lives of ancient philosophers.
introduction: From Diogenes Laertius, Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers — our main source for the lives of ancient philosophers.

translation.text: Socrates was the son of a stonemason and a midwife. He served in the army at Potidaea and Delium. He was tried and condemned to death in 399 BCE. He died drinking the hemlock with calm philosophical conversation.
